agentFile.format: TOML (one custom agent per standalone .toml file; layers/overrides normal session config)
agentFile.bodyIs: There is no separate Markdown body. The agent definition IS the TOML file; the system prompt/instructions are expressed via the 'developer_instructions' string field, not a free-form document body.

description.description: Human-facing guidance describing when Codex should use this agent.

developer_instructions.description: Core behavior definition for the subagent; this is where the system prompt / instructions are expressed.

nickname_candidates.allowed: non-empty list of unique display names
nickname_candidates.description: Pool of display nicknames for the spawned agent thread.

model.default: inherits from parent session if omitted
model.description: Model the subagent uses; falls back to the parent session model when unset.

model_reasoning_effort.allowed: minimal | low | medium | high | xhigh (per config-reference)
model_reasoning_effort.default: inherits from parent session if omitted

sandbox_mode.default: inherits from parent session if omitted
sandbox_mode.description: Filesystem/network restriction level; can override the parent session (e.g. tighten to read-only).

mcp_servers.description: MCP server configuration scoped to this subagent.

skills.config.allowed: list of skill config entries (path, enabled)
skills.config.description: Per-skill overrides available to the subagent.

bashInjection.description: No bash/shell command injection token documented for the subagent definition file. developer_instructions is a plain string.

fileInjection.description: No file-injection token (e.g. @file) documented for subagent definition files.

argumentTokens.description: No positional ($1-$9) or $ARGUMENTS argument tokens documented inside subagent definition files. (Such tokens exist only for the separate, deprecated ~/.codex/prompts custom-prompt feature, not for subagents.)

toolReference.description: No explicit in-content tool-reference token documented. Subagents do not have a frontmatter 'tools' allow/deny field on the subagents page; tool access derives from session/MCP/skills config. unverified whether enabled_tools/disabled_tools apply.

mcpReference.description: MCP servers are referenced declaratively via the mcp_servers config table in the subagent file (not via an in-content @mention token).

variableTemplating.description: Variable templating is documented only for CSV batch spawning (spawn_agents_on_csv), where {column_name} placeholders in the instruction parameter are substituted per CSV row. No general-purpose templating is documented inside the subagent definition file body itself.

agentMention.description: No @mention syntax for invoking/referencing other agents. Subagents are spawned explicitly via natural-language request to Codex or managed via the /agent slash command; auto-delegation is not supported.

invocation.description: Codex only spawns a subagent when the user explicitly asks (e.g. 'spawn two agents', 'delegate this in parallel'). The /agent slash command switches between, inspects, steers, stops, or closes agent threads. No automatic delegation, no @mention, no per-agent slash command.

skillResolution.resolveBy: directory-scan (explicit prompt invocation or implicit by skill description)

referencedInAgentDefinition.binds: TOML subagent may carry per-skill config OVERRIDES, but skills themselves are auto-discovered by directory, NOT bound by name in the agent def

searchPaths.path: $CWD/.agents/skills (every dir from cwd up to repo root)

skillResolution.agentsDirNote: PRIMARY discovery is the vendor-neutral .agents/skills/ tree (cwd->repo root), plus codex-native ~/.codex/skills.
